slides.title: The small talk that *adds years*
slides.sub: Brief chats with neighbors, baristas and strangers are quietly good for you. Here is why they matter.

slides.title: Weak ties still count
slides.body: Not every relationship has to be deep. The light, familiar exchanges of your day are called *weak ties*, and they pull real weight.

slides.title: Micro-doses of belonging
slides.body: A short, friendly chat is a small signal that you belong somewhere. Those signals *can buffer everyday stress* better than we expect.

slides.title: More chats, more wellbeing
slides.body: Studies link days with more casual social contact to *higher daily wellbeing*. The effect holds even for brief, ordinary moments.

slides.title: Connection and a longer life
slides.body: Strong social connection is linked to longer, healthier lives. Weak ties widen that web *without asking much of you*.

slides.title: Say one extra thing
slides.body: Greet the dog walker. Thank the barista by name. One small line a day *can lift your mood* and your sense of place.

slides.title: Track the habits behind *a longer life*
slides.body: Vibe Health turns your sleep, food, movement and steps into one daily Health Score, so the routines that support connection and longevity stay easy to see.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The barista, the neighbor, the stranger in line. Those tiny chats are doing more for you than you think. 🌿💬

Weak ties are micro-doses of belonging that can buffer stress and lift the wellbeing tied to a longer life. One small line a day adds up. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your daily habits into one simple Health Score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Small Talk With Strangers Can Help You Live Longer
